title: Boy, 16, arrested on suspicion of woman's murder

input.summary: A woman in her 60s died at a house in Burnage, Manchester, after reports of an assault, police say
input.evidence_text: Boy, 16, arrested on suspicion of woman's murder - BBC News Image caption, A woman in her 60s died at a house in Burnage, police say Published 17 minutes ago A 16-year-old boy has been arrested on suspicion of murder after a woman died at a house in a suburban street. Officers were called to Ivylea Road in Burnage, south Manchester, after reports of an assault at about 08:00 BST on Wednesday, Greater Manchester Police said. The force said a woman in her 60s was pronounced dead at the scene. Her cause of death is not yet known. GMP said the teenager remained in custody for questioning. title: Drug mules flooding north of England face tougher penalties

input.lead: The National Crime Agency and Thai authorities have launched a crackdown on drug smugglers
input.summary: The National Crime Agency and Thai authorities have launched a crackdown on drug smugglers
input.evidence_text: British "mules" caught smuggling cannabis from Thailand face jail and a charge for every kilo they are carrying under new tough measures to combat a drug trafficking "explosion" The National Crime Agency (NCA) warning came after a BBC North West investigation heard how gangs were making huge profits sending couriers from Bangkok to the UK with loads in suitcases destined for the streets of the cities and towns of the north of England The agency is joining Thai Authorities and the Home Office to combat the surge Anyone caught trying to fly the drug out of Thailand will have to pay Thai Customs 30,000 Baht – the equivalent to around £68 per kilogram of cannabis – to leave the country The number of smuggling attempts from Thailand has continued to rise sharply – despite efforts to tighten relaxed laws there around cultivation – as dealers attempt to cash in on huge demand for super-strength weed Last year, 976 air passengers were caught arriving in the UK with cannabis, a massive increase from the 142 arrested in 2023

final_reason: Status: drop

title: Manchester student's dismay as family heirloom saxophone stolen

input.lead: Conor Parker-Delves is an aspiring musician but now faces putting his career on hold
input.summary: Conor Parker-Delves is an aspiring musician but now faces putting his career on hold
input.evidence_text: An aspiring musician has told of the "horrible" moment he realised the saxophone his late grandfather gave him had been stolen in a robbery Conor Parker-Delves spent his last night at his university house in Longsight, Manchester, on 29 June, after studying saxophone performance at Royal Northern College of Music for four years But when he stepped outside the following morning, the car packed with his possessions had been opened, and two saxophones worth a total of £12,000 had been taken Parker-Delves said one of the items stolen was key to his family's memory of his granddad, and that raising the money to replace the instruments was "nearly impossible" The gold tenor Selma Mark VI first belonged to Parker-Delves' grandfather, Frank Parker, who was "massively obsessed with saxophone and jazz music" "He passed away when I was very young and so I've been incredibly lucky to play on it," Parker-Delves told BBC Radio Manchester

title: UK government will change law so grooming gang ringleader can be deported

input.lead: The home secretary is expected to set out on Monday how she plans to amend the 1971 Immigration Act
input.summary: The home secretary is expected to set out on Monday how she plans to amend the 1971 Immigration Act
input.evidence_text: UK government will change law so grooming gang ringleader can be deported - BBC News Image source, GMP Image caption, Shabir Ahmed led a grooming gang that abused girls as young as 12 By Jack Fenwick , Political correspondent and Anna Lamche Published 8 July 2026 The home secretary will change the law so the freed ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang can be deported, the BBC has been told. As first reported by the Telegraph, Shabana Mahmood is expected to set out on Monday how she plans to amend the 1971 Immigration Act which currently stops Shabir Ahmed being removed from the UK. Ahmed, 73, was jailed for 22 years in August 2012 for a number of child sexual offences including rape. He was released on licence last week. Known to his victims as "Daddy", Ahmed had dual British-Pakistani citizenship but was stripped of the former following his 2012 conviction. It is not known how long it would take to change the law but one government source suggested it could potentially be up to a year. At the time of his release, his victims were told he could not be deported to Pakistan due to the 55-year-old Immigration Act, which bars the removal of any Commonwealth citizen who arrived in the UK before 1973 and had been in the country for five years. After leaving prison, Ahmed was sent to 24-hour staffed accommodation and fitted with a GPS electronically monitored tag. He is subject to strict licence conditions following his release, the Home Office said, including exclusion zones, an electronically monitored curfew and the requirements of the sex offenders register. Some of his victims said they were "frightened" by his release, and they felt "unsafe". While he is in the UK, the government has said any breach of Ahmed's strict licence conditions would result in him being immediat

input.summary: The arrests were made as part of a "complex investigation" into the University of Greater Manchester

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Newlyweds from St Helens celebrate with thousands of Pitbull fans

input.lead: Kym and Tyler got married in Warrington before dashing to see the final day of the Lytham Festival
input.summary: Kym and Tyler got married in Warrington before dashing to see the final day of the Lytham Festival
input.evidence_text: A couple who went to see the American rapper Pitbull perform at a festival on their wedding day say their 15-hour celebrations were "a bit mad" Kym Sutcliffe and Tyler Scarisbrick, from St Helens, got married at St Margaret's and All Hallows Church in Warrington on Sunday After a quick pit stop at a local pub, they saw Pitbull aka Mr Worldwide headline the Lytham Festival's final day before rounding everything off by finding a bar to watch England beat Mexico in the last 16 of the World Cup Pitbull fans are known for emulating the star's bald appearance at his gigs. Kym told BBC Radio Lancashire : "I had my wedding dress on, and my bald cap, and my goatee, I must have looked a right picture!" The couple had only been engaged for about six weeks and brought forward their wedding plans in the hope that an ill family member could attend Unfortunately, their relative's health took a turn for the worse and they were unable to make the wedding, which Kym said had made the occasion "bittersweet"

title: Cannabis smuggler knows most of his drugs mules 'will get caught'

input.lead: A smuggler taunts the government even though the UK Border Force is seizing record amounts of cannabis
input.summary: A smuggler taunts the government even though the UK Border Force is seizing record amounts of cannabis
input.evidence_text: Drugs gangs are flooding the north of England with cheap cannabis grown overseas and sold to mimic slickly marketed, legal marijuana from the United States, a BBC investigation has learned A thriving black market for "Cali weed" - high-potency strains originally cultivated in licensed dispensaries in California - has prompted an unprecedented wave of smuggling from dealers growing it in countries including Thailand One dealer boasted he made massive profits importing cannabis and described how he sent drug couriers from Thailand to the UK, even though he knew "most will get caught" According to Home Office figures seen by the BBC, Border Force officials last year seized more than 167,000kg of herbal cannabis with an estimated street value of more than £2bn - the highest amount ever recorded and up 52% from 2024 The haul - equivalent to two fully loaded large passenger jets - included 28 tonnes of cannabis flown in suitcases by 800 couriers who were arrested by the National Crime Agency (NCA) Suppliers are running industrial-sized operations in parts of the world where laws governing the cultivation of cannabis have been relaxed - including Thailand, Canada and parts of the US - and illegally importing multiple-kilogram loads to the UK for "huge profits"

title: Pensioner fighting for life after Trafford stabbing

input.summary: Officers were called to Partington on Tuesday evening after hearing reports of a stabbing
input.evidence_text: Pensioner fighting for life after Trafford stabbing - BBC News Image source, Google Image caption, Officers found the man with stab wounds at Heather Walk, in Partington By Dan Wareing North West Published 8 July 2026 A 72-year-old man has been taken to hospital in critical condition after a stabbing in an alleyway. It happened on Tuesday at 18:05 BST, on Heather Walk, Partington, Greater Manchester. Officers arrived at the scene to find the pensioner with stab wounds, before a woman was arrested on suspicion of causing grievous bodily harm and a man on suspicion of assisting an offender, Greater Manchester Police said. The force said the incident was isolated and posed "no wider threat to the public". input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

input.evidence_text: Drugs gangs are flooding the north of England with cheap cannabis grown overseas and sold to mimic slickly marketed, legal marijuana from the United States, a BBC investigation has learned A thriving black market for "Cali weed" - high-potency strains originally cultivated in licensed dispensaries in California - has prompted an unprecedented wave of smuggling from dealers growing it in countries including Thailand One dealer boasted he made massive profits importing cannabis and described how he sent drug couriers from Thailand to the UK, even though he knew "most will get caught" According to Home Office figures seen by the BBC, Border Force officials last year seized more than 167,000kg of herbal cannabis with an estimated street value of more than £2bn - the highest amount ever recorded and up 52% from 2024 The haul - equivalent to two fully loaded large passenger jets - included 28 tonnes of cannabis flown in suitcases by 800 couriers who were arrested by the National Crime Agency (NCA) Suppliers are running industrial-sized operations in parts of the world where laws governing the cultivation of cannabis have been relaxed - including Thailand, Canada and parts of the US - and illegally importing multiple-kilogram loads to the UK for "huge profits"

input.summary: Within Chetham's ancient walls, Greater Manchester asked for honesty Chetham’s has witnessed centuries of upheaval, debate and reinvention. On Wednesday, as six contenders for the Greater Manchester mayoralty took the stage, the audience delivered a s News Rob Williams Features a
input.evidence_text: Within Chetham's ancient walls, Greater Manchester asked for honesty Chetham’s has witnessed centuries of upheaval, debate and reinvention. On Wednesday, as six contenders for the Greater Manchester mayoralty took the stage, the audience delivered a s News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 20:02, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:08, 09 Jul 2026 Tucked away behind the bustle of Cathedral Gardens and the modern skyline of Manchester city centre , the ancient buildings of the Chetham School of Music have stood in the heart of t Their sandstone walls have witnessed plague and prosperity, civil war and industrial revolution, the rise of Manchester as the world's first industrial city and its reinvention as one of Europe's fastest-growing urban regions. Every generation has left its mark here Founded in 1421 as a college for priests before becoming Chetham's Hospital and later one of the world's leading specialist music schools, the site represents something uniquely Mancunian: a place where history and modernity exist comfortably side by side. The medieval courtyard There can be few more fitting places to stage a political debate than Chetham's School of Music. Marx and Engels fashioned their plans to bring down global capitalism here Some different ideas have become popular since

They're about who worked the room, who looked comfortable under pressure, who landed their arguments, who survived the toughest questions and who left wishing for just ten more seconds. Here's how we saw it Coggins was a confident voice on the panel and was clearly popular with the audience and at ease. Her framing of the debate was consistent throughout. Labour has let down Greater Manchester overall while building up the city centre with economic deals that have not benefited the Strongest moment: Tackling Reform. There’s not much around here anymore, no pubs or anything. It really is a lifeline for peopl The charity organisation was previously funded by the Lottery Fund, but after losing out during the bidding process this year, is due to run out of funding before the end of the year. That means all of its activities – which includes the coffee morning, film screenings, and a lin The impact on the area’s local elderly could be ‘devastating’, according to volunteers and visitors “Without this, you’re isolated,” Ms Jones told the LDRS. “This morning I walked out of my house and got a taxi here because I knew staying at home would make my mental health suffer. I’m in a really dark place at the moment, but in here I know I’m okay.” Ms Jones cares for her daughter full-time, and says even though they’re constantly together, the caring responsibilities can leave her feeling cut-off from the outside world

input.evidence_text: Within Chetham's ancient walls, Greater Manchester asked for honesty Chetham’s has witnessed centuries of upheaval, debate and reinvention. On Wednesday, as six contenders for the Greater Manchester mayoralty took the stage, the audience delivered a s News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 20:02, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:08, 09 Jul 2026 Tucked away behind the bustle of Cathedral Gardens and the modern skyline of Manchester city centre , the ancient buildings of the Chetham School of Music have stood in the heart of t Their sandstone walls have witnessed plague and prosperity, civil war and industrial revolution, the rise of Manchester as the world's first industrial city and its reinvention as one of Europe's fastest-growing urban regions. Every generation has left its mark here Founded in 1421 as a college for priests before becoming Chetham's Hospital and later one of the world's leading specialist music schools, the site represents something uniquely Mancunian: a place where history and modernity exist comfortably side by side. The medieval courtyard There can be few more fitting places to stage a political debate than Chetham's School of Music. Marx and Engels fashioned their plans to bring down global capitalism here Some different ideas have become popular since

It’s also a way of encouraging families to do the school run using public transport or active travel such as cycling Other benefits include improving air quality and stopping ‘inconsiderate parking’ around schools Most school streets are enforced through barriers and volunteers, but council bosses hope using cameras will reduce traffic outside schools and make the streets feel safer for families and kids

Her granddaughter Kay said the driver of a taxi she was getting in accidentally drove off while her foot was still out of the car, causing her to fall to the ground Linda severely fractured her back in two places and was regularly in hospital for appointments when her health then continued to deteriorate Speaking to the M.E.N., her granddaughter Kay said: "She had just been going shopping when she fell out of the taxi. input.evidence_text: Within Chetham's ancient walls, Greater Manchester asked for honesty Chetham’s has witnessed centuries of upheaval, debate and reinvention. On Wednesday, as six contenders for the Greater Manchester mayoralty took the stage, the audience delivered a s News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 20:02, 08 Jul 2026 Updated 06:08, 09 Jul 2026 Tucked away behind the bustle of Cathedral Gardens and the modern skyline of Manchester city centre , the ancient buildings of the Chetham School of Music have stood in the heart of t Their sandstone walls have witnessed plague and prosperity, civil war and industrial revolution, the rise of Manchester as the world's first industrial city and its reinvention as one of Europe's fastest-growing urban regions. Every generation has left its mark here Founded in 1421 as a college for priests before becoming Chetham's Hospital and later one of the world's leading specialist music schools, the site represents something uniquely Mancunian: a place where history and modernity exist comfortably side by side. The medieval courtyard There can be few more fitting places to stage a political debate than Chetham's School of Music. Marx and Engels fashioned their plans to bring down global capitalism here Some different ideas have become popular since

She was just 28 and a mother-of-two when she was hanged at Holloway Prison The Ministry of Justice said an application for a pardon was brought by four of Ellis's grandchildren The application, added the MoJ, 'presented that her responsibility was profoundly shaped by domestic abuse, trauma and circumstances that were never properly recognised at her trial' Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy, speaking in the House of Commons on Wednesday, said: "I have the honour to say that His Majesty the King has accepted our advice to grant Ruth Ellis a conditional pardon, the last woman to be hanged in the United Kingdom

input.summary: Bury The £1.5m plan to ready a Bury park for 150 new homes The land is 'strewn' with litter and plagued by anti-social behaviour Comments News Hannah Richardson 10:23, 08 Jul 2026 View Image A new town centre community could be built at Pyramid Park i Bury’s Pyramid Park has lain
input.evidence_text: Bury The £1.5m plan to ready a Bury park for 150 new homes The land is 'strewn' with litter and plagued by anti-social behaviour Comments News Hannah Richardson 10:23, 08 Jul 2026 View Image A new town centre community could be built at Pyramid Park i Bury’s Pyramid Park has lain derelict and overgrown for ‘well over’ 15 years. Now, Bury council is taking steps to give it a new lease of life The land, off Market Street , was the home of the Knowsley Street railway station and its associated goods yard. The line was closed by British Rail in 1970 and the land reclaimed by Bury council which turned it into a public park But low usage, anti-social behaviour and access issues led town hall bosses to decommission the site in 2010. In the years since, the condition of the park has worsened, with the council saying it is overgrown, ‘strewn with litter’ and continues to attract unsavoury behaviour In 2023, the council’s leaders approved the disposal of the land with a view to housing being built there. Plans show around 150 new homes could ultimately sit on the land Before a developer can take on the project, however, the site needs to be ‘remediated’, ‘derisked’ and prepared for development. Bury council’s cabinet is now set to approve a contract worth £1.55m with Conlon Construction Ltd to carry out that work

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

input.evidence_text: Energy costs have become one of the more awkward line items for businesses across Greater Manchester For offices, warehouses, manufacturers, hospitality venues, retail units and leisure operators, electricity is no longer just a background overhead. It is now a serious commercial variable, affecting margins, pricing, cash flow and long-term planning That is one reason solar power is moving from a “nice to have” sustainability measure to a more practical business investment Across the North West, more companies are looking at whether their roofs, sites and car parks can generate electricity rather than simply buy it from the grid. The argument is no longer only environmental. title: A dish best served cold: A TV historian sued us to reveal our sources - and lost

input.summary: Laurence Westgaph waged a legal campaign to find out who had spoken to one of our reporters. A year later, we met him in court
input.evidence_text: Laurence Westgaph waged a legal campaign to find out who had spoken to one of our reporters. A year later, we met him in court On the first day of the trial earlier this month, TV historian Laurence Westgaph was asked to turn to page 255 of the hearing bundle: the thick ringbinder of documents that chronicle a year-long legal battle that was now, finally, coming to a head in court Westgaph, a well-known figure in Liverpool who has presented history shows on the BBC, was asked to read a Facebook post he’d published about me in July last year. He began to read it out “I served the big busta, Mr Herrmann this week…” Our barrister, Claire Overman, stopped him. She hadn’t meant for him to read the post out loud. Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. Claire, representing Mill Media, asked him about the dish “best served cold”

title: Let's get metaphysical: could an ex-philosopher be the new Burnham?

input.lead: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors.
input.summary: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize
input.evidence_text: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize Before politics, one interest dominated the life of Geraldine Coggins, leader of the Trafford Greens, candidate for Greater Manchester mayor, and that was metaphysical nihilism. The now-politician harbours a lifelong love of philosophy, studying the subject up and down the UK and What is metaphysical nihilism? When I ask her, Coggins seems reluctant to discuss it, directing me instead towards the book’s synopsis. “The content is very abstract,” she tells me, “and doesn’t affect my worldview now.” Maybe Coggins is trying to draw a distinction between the r And what a time in the Green Party it’s been. When I meet her, drinking matcha outside a glass-fronted MediaCity cafe, she runs me through the last decade-and-a-half in her Irish lilt: a councillor for eight years, a group leader for four; becoming one of Trafford's first Green c Coggins possesses a demeanour at once warm and authoritative, and it’s this unlikely mix that I’m told helped her win the favour of Altrincham’s electorate in 2018, when, alongside councillor Daniel Jerrome, she made history Two seats were vacant in the ward that year, and the pair stood as the Green candidates. Jerrome recalls a febrile atmosphere on polling day, with neither opposition parties nor the press able to predict what was to come

title: Chezdan was young, gay and living in a council house in Bolton. He also wanted to be on TV — at any cost

input.summary: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices
input.evidence_text: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices. When he reached the There followed the ghoulies, four of them, a Nickelodeon-troupe of pasty white zombies waddling out from the shrubbery, followed by serial prankster and TV host Ross Lee (who would eventually hit dizzying heights with the Keith Lemon Sketch Show). When we got to the studio in Lon Chezdan Mills had his own post-TV fame realisation that same year, 2009. His came at a bus stop The show Chezdan had been on had a viewership more than a touch higher than Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . At least, while I don’t think Chezdan can recall seeing me and my brother standing proud before the Scareometer, I distinctly recall watching him, arguing with an irate Pastor on BBC At that time, British reality TV operated under a defining principle. The key was to be as nasty as possible to ordinary people, and to make them appear as ludicrous as possible for the entertainment of the viewing public. Bonus points could be earned if they were poor, or 16 Chezdan was 16. He came from Bolton. He wanted — at just about any cost — to be on the TV

input.evidence_text: The incident happened on Bradgate Road last month Share on WhatsApp Share via Email A convicted murderer has pleaded guilty to kidnapping a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint and subjecting her to an eight-hour ordeal in his car Paul Sharpe, 56, of Portwood, Stockport, entered guilty pleas at Minshull Street Crown Court on Thursday to six offences: kidnapping, sexual assault, engaging in sexual activity in the presence of a child, making threats with a bladed article, committing an offence with intent to Sharpe approached the girl on Bradgate Road in Altrincham at around 7.30pm on May 18, threatened her with a knife, put her in a headlock and forced her into his car. He told her that her father owed him money He drove her to a car park near Manchester Airport, where he tied her wrists and ankles with a necktie. Over the following hours he moved between different car parks, forcing her to drink alcohol and sexually assaulting her The girl’s mother reported her missing, prompting a major police investigation. Extensive CCTV enquiries identified Sharpe’s vehicle, and he was arrested in a hotel car park in Knutsford at around 3.40am the following morning with the girl still in the car

input.evidence_text: The special charter train will set off on Saturday 22nd August Share on WhatsApp Share via Email Rail travellers along the Mid Cheshire Line are being invited to enjoy a unique day out in August aboard The Retro Cestrian Bard, a special charter train bound for Stratford-upon-Avon Promoted by the Mid Cheshire Rail Users' Association (MCRUA) and operated by Retro Railtours, the excursion on Saturday 22nd August will pick up passengers at Altrincham, Knutsford, Northwich and Chester before heading south to the famous birthplace of William Shakespeare The return leg will take a different, more scenic route, threading through the Malvern Hills and along the Welsh Marches, giving passengers a chance to take in some of Britain's finest railway landscapes The train itself promises to be as much a part of the day out as the destination. Comfortable Mk3 air-conditioned coaches will be hauled by Class 37 diesel locomotives, with every passenger guaranteed a reserved seat Once in Stratford-upon-Avon, passengers will have around three hours to explore the town's historic streets, riverside walks, theatres and the many attractions linked to Shakespeare's life and work

title: Manchester puts young people's voices at the heart of health

input.lead: Young people are set to be at the heart of policymaking when it comes to people’s health and wellbeing in Manchester
input.summary: Young people are set to be at the heart of policymaking when it comes to people’s health and wellbeing in Manchester
input.evidence_text: Manchester puts young people’s voices at the heart of health | Manchester City Council Manchester puts young people’s voices at the heart of health Wednesday 8 July 2026 Young people are set to be at the heart of policymaking when it comes to people’s health and wellbeing in Manchester. This follows the publication this week of the Public Health Annual Report for Manchester. This expansive document shines a light on the health and wellbeing of everyone living in the city, and how the Council plans to tackle issues such as health inequality, access to services, and how to help people live healthier lives. Entitled “Our Right to Health” one of its key highlights is the importance of putting young people's voices at the heart of shaping services, policies and future priorities. This comes just weeks after Manchester celebrated being recognised as a UNICEF UK Child Friendly City, reinforcing its commitment to ensuring that children’s rights are built into local decision making and that young people have meaningful opportunities to influence change. The work has also received national recognition, with the Association of Directors of Public Health giving the report a special mention for tackling a complex and challenging topic — underlining the importance of focusing on young people’s health and lived experiences. Putting young people’s voices first The report makes clear that young people are best placed to describe the realities of growing up in Manchester today, and their insights are critical to designing effective support. From concerns about the cost of living and access to healthy food, to pressures on mental health and feelings of safety in their communities, the report captures what matters most to young people — and sets out how partners across the city can respond

title: More than a game: football's role in one family's fostering journey - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.summary: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.evidence_text: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care Lauren (46) and her husband Danny Sullivan (52) have fostered for Rochdale Borough Council since 2012 and currently care for 5 young lads aged 12 to 21, alongside their 9-year-old son, Daniel Their home is very busy as you can imagine and ‘the washing machine is non-stop’ Lauren jokes. Each child has their own interests, including things like the gym and gaming, but football is what stands out as the cement in their daily routines From training and coaching on weekdays to refereeing and playing matches at the weekend, the house is football mad and it’s their passion for the sport which connects them as a family and what has helped the lads to create a strong brotherly bond “They’ll all come home after school, college or work, and Daniel will ask them for a game,” Lauren said, with a pair of football nets visible outside from the living room “It’s so lovely to see the smiles on their faces when they’re all playing together in the back garden.”

input.summary: Press release — 23 June 2026 11:45 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download Bury Council is moving forward with its plan to formally close Six Town Housing Limited (STH), the organisation previously responsible for managing much of the borough’s social housing In Fe
input.evidence_text: Press release — 23 June 2026 11:45 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download Bury Council is moving forward with its plan to formally close Six Town Housing Limited (STH), the organisation previously responsible for managing much of the borough’s social housing In February 2024, the council brought most of the 7,800 properties back under its direct management, but STH continued to own and manage 117 specialist social rent properties Last September the council decided to begin the process to close STH and secure a long-term future for these remaining homes The council has been undertaking detailed work to review the future of the STH portfolio. This has included an in-depth financial analysis which concluded that bringing these homes back into direct council ownership might not be the best viable option in the long term No decision has been made. The council is keeping all options open and is exploring the potential for these homes to be transferred to another Registered Provider of Social Housing (a housing association) as one possible way to ensure services continue to be delivered in a stable

title: Wigan's new market to open in spring 2027

input.lead: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spr
input.summary: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spr
input.evidence_text: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spring 2027, allowing time for the council and traders to shape a venue that supports existing businesses and offers the best possible visitor experience in the town centre. The timetable also means traders will have the opportunity to enjoy Following the decision for the market to be operated directly by Wigan Council, additional work is now being undertaken to focus on developing an operating model that works for the people at the heart of the market This insight is now feeding into the final layout of the building’s internal design, ensuring the market’s long-standing businesses remain at the heart of one of the borough’s most significant regeneration projects Wigan Market forms the centrepiece of the wider Fettlers development and will bring the market back to the heart of Wigan town centre, creating a vibrant destination where people can shop, eat, meet and spend time together

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.evidence_text: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact across the globe The Blue Plaque was unveiled at a ceremony on Park Road, Golborne, attended by the Mayor of Wigan Borough Councillor Chris Ready, cabinet portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods, said: “Dr John Garton’s work has helped transform farming practices and improve sustainability, making him a significant figure not just locally, but across the world “The Blue Plaque scheme is designed to celebrate the impact residents of our borough have made and Dr Garton’s legacy and influence in the world of agriculture will live on.”

title: Flood risk investment to support residents in Hindley, Platt Bridge and Abram

input.lead: Wigan Council will commit £3m to a flood defence scheme that could unlock further major funding Ahead of public consultation this summer, the local authority is hoping its contribution will attract wider support The scheme would see flood risk prevention measures tailored for com
input.summary: Wigan Council will commit £3m to a flood defence scheme that could unlock further major funding Ahead of public consultation this summer, the local authority is hoping its contribution will attract wider support The scheme would see flood risk prevention measures tailored for com
input.evidence_text: Wigan Council will commit £3m to a flood defence scheme that could unlock further major funding Ahead of public consultation this summer, the local authority is hoping its contribution will attract wider support The scheme would see flood risk prevention measures tailored for communities in Hindley, Platt Bridge and Abram, areas recently affected by river flooding The consultation will provide an opportunity to assess the available options and to learn more about how the projects will progress Councillor Nazia Rehman, Leader of Wigan Council, said: “This commitment sets out a clear timeline for how we will support our residents, many of whom have been impacted by recent flooding “We have been consistently making the case on their behalf; listening to residents, working closely with partners such as Defra and the Environment Agency, and lobbying for the funding and support our communities need

input.evidence_text: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo This piece belongs to a wider series called ‘Desire Me.’ It aims to translate unspoken feelings and moments of desire that occur between us The focus is on longing, on reaching out and on instances of hopeful touch that happen in the early stages of a relationship. title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.
input.summary: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, original
input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. In 2023, the club made history by winning the Treble - lifting the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League all in one incredible season. It was a feat achieved by only one other English club before them, and it cemented City’s place among the footballing elite. After playing at Maine Road for 80 years, Manchester City relocated in 2003 to the Etihad Stadium in East Manchester - a move that marked a new era for the club. The stadium was converted into a world-class football ground and has since become central to the team’s rise to the top of English and European football. With a capacity of over 53,000, state-of-the-art facilities, and a great atmosphere on matchdays, the Etihad Stadium is not only the club’s home but also a key part of Manchester’s sporting and cultural id

title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.
input.summary: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, original
input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. In 2023, the club made history by winning the Treble - lifting the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League all in one incredible season. It was a feat achieved by only one other English club before them, and it cemented City’s place among the footballing elite. After playing at Maine Road for 80 years, Manchester City relocated in 2003 to the Etihad Stadium in East Manchester - a move that marked a new era for the club. The stadium was converted into a world-class football ground and has since become central to the team’s rise to the top of English and European football. title: Спектакль "Скамейка"

input.lead: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра?
input.summary: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным
input.evidence_text: Спектакль «Скамейка» в Лондоне | Алексей Панин | 29 ноября 2026 :: EventFirst Home double_arrow Events double_arrow Спектакль "Скамейка" On sale Спектакль "Скамейка" schedule 29 Nov 2026 Sunday, 19:00 location_on Logan Hall 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL balcony 6:00 pm gpp_maybe NO REFUNDS Buy On MAP Buy Tickets Logan Hall £30.00 Select Logan Hall £35.00 Select Logan Hall £40.00 Select Logan Hall £45.00 Select Logan Hall £50.00 Select Logan Hall £55.00 Select Logan Hall £60.00 Select Logan Hall £65.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £70.00 Select Logan Hall £80.00 Select Logan Hall £85.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £90.00 Select Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон. 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру. Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться. На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё. “Скамейка” — спектакль, после которого хочется немного помолчать и еще долго думать о главном. Если вам не хватает настоящего театра — этот вечер нельзя пропустить. Количество билетов ограничено. Продолжительность спектакля — 2 часа с антрактом. settings Loading venue plan... shopping_cart Your tickets: 0

input.evidence_text: Porta and Tartuffe have spent months building a 10 course Spanish-inspired menu of Gildas, grilled seafood, chicken thighs and giant open-fire paella By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 8 July 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile There are few restaurants that inspire the kind of loyalty Porta does. The sort of place where you go in for a vermouth and a couple of plates, then realise two hours later that you’ve ordered half the menu and are seriously considering another That easy-going, no-fuss approach has made the walk-in-only tapas bar a favourite across its four sites in Chester, Altrincham, Salford and West Didsbury. Founded by brothers Ben and Joe Wright, Porta takes its cues from the neighbourhood bars of Spain: short menus, good produce, This July, Porta is leaving the comfort of its own dining rooms to join resident chefs Tartuffe at Torr Vale Mill for the next Open Fire Dining Club . It’s a collaboration that has been in the works for months Tartuffe’s Gary Weir and Porta’s executive chef José Garzón have spent that time trading ideas and finding the right meeting point between the two kitchens. title: Manchester cybersecurity firm plans £185 million shareholder return following triple disposal

input.lead: Manchester-based cybersecurity expert NCC Group has announced plans to return £185 million to its shareholders following the completion of the £253 million sale of its Escode division and two other disposals since 2024 which bring total receipts to £349m
input.summary: Manchester-based cybersecurity expert NCC Group has announced plans to return £185 million to its shareholders following the completion of the £253 million sale of its Escode division and two other disposals since 2024 which bring total receipts to £349m
input.evidence_text: Manchester cybersecurity firm plans £185 million shareholder return following triple disposal - Prolific North Skip to content Agency Blackpool printshop enters wide format market with £30k+ installation Agency Liverpool agency sees off 20 rivals to land Devon family safety campaign Brand Sheffield Wednesday FC names new front-of-shirt sponsor Manchester cybersecurity firm plans £185 million shareholder return following triple disposal Tech by Chris Newbould July 7, 2026 Manchester-based cybersecurity expert NCC Group has announced plans to return £185 million to its shareholders following the completion of the £253 million sale of its Escode division and two other disposals since 2024 which bring total receipts to £349m. The proposal follows an earlier £40 million share buyback and includes a capital reduction that will create approximately £225 million of distributable reserves. Subject to shareholder approval and court confirmation, the company intends to launch a £170 million tender offer at 145 pence per share alongside an additional £15 million share buyback. The tender offer represents an 11% premium to NCC Group’s most recent closing share price and, if fully subscribed, could result in the repurchase and cancellation of up to 41.2% of the company’s issued share capital. Such a move would significantly reduce the group’s equity base. The board has confirmed that company directors will not participate in the tender offer and has chosen not to recommend whether shareholders should accept the offer, leaving individual investors to decide whether to realise immediate value or retain their investment for future growth. The square itself ha From here, the tour takes you through the city’s heart, down the colourful streets of the Gay Village and the calming Sackville Gardens. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . Housed in a beautiful sandsto During all this walking, your guide will point out local street art, hidden corners and stories you’d easily miss on your own. input.evidence_text: Discover Campfield Market — a modern city market in the heart of Manchester. Every Saturday, explore 70+ independent traders, street food pop-ups, local produce inside the historic Campfield buildings Campfield Market is Manchester’s weekly city market, bringing together street food, local produce and artisanal makers from the city’s best independent traders Inspired by traditional European markets, Campfield Market stretches from under the iconic glass roof out into the streets beyond Now reaching into St John’s and right down Grape Street, it transforms the area into a full kilometre loop of market stalls, street food and independent retail Each week you’ll find a rotating line-up of street food traders alongside European-style food and drink, quality produce and a carefully curated mix of artisan makers With over 100 traders landing every Saturday and a line-up that changes week on week, it’s built for wandering, repeat visits and discovering something new every time A weekly market that supports independents, serves the local community and brings people together every Saturday Campfield Market | Manchester’s New City Market 0 . . . input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: Watch our Asia Tour 2026 matches on CITY+

input.lead: Watch our Asia Tour 2026 matches on CITY+
input.summary: City first head to Hong Kong to face Italian champions Inter in a 12:30 (UK) kick-off on Saturday 1 August. After that we go to Seoul to play a K League All-Stars XI at 12:00 (UK) on Wednesday 5 August before meeting Atletico Madrid at the same time on Sunday 9 August
input.evidence_text: City first head to Hong Kong to face Italian champions Inter in a 12:30 (UK) kick-off on Saturday 1 August. After that we go to Seoul to play a K League All-Stars XI at 12:00 (UK) on Wednesday 5 August before meeting Atletico Madrid at the same time on Sunday 9 August. It’s set to act as ideal preparation for our 2026/27 campaign, before returning home ahead of the Community Shield clash with Arsenal. With Enzo Maresca taking charge, every member of the squad will be seeking to put their best foot forward and stake a claim to be in the starting lineup come the outset of the domestic season. Given the calibre of the opposition, it’s also a great time to see how we stack up ahead of yet another tilt at silverware domestically and in the Champions League. As well as that, we’ll see the fervent support City have gained in Asia over recent years, having last been to this part in the world in 2023 and sold out stadiums in Japan and South Korea. We’ll be there to cover it every step of the way, with presenters and legends on the ground in Hong Kong and Seoul to give their take on how City are shaping up. And the best place to watch the games as they happen is right here on mancity.com and our official app. To do so, you’ll need a CITY+ subscription, which is currently priced at £34.99 annually or £9.99 a month. Or you can unlock CITY+ as part of our Premium Membership (£65 annually), which brings together the best of City in one membership. *Please note, the CITY+ broadcast of these games will be unavailable in very limited territories. Our meeting with Inter will not be available in Italy or China. Both of our games against K League All-Stars and Atletico Madrid will be unavailable in South Korea, while the Atletico clash will also not be accessible in Spain. Buy your 2026/27

The defending European champions are yet to concede a goal in the tournament so far, having played five games to reach this point. Our man Rodri, who won Best Player at Euro 2024 before lifting the Ballon d’Or a few months later, has been captain throughout the run and was named Player of the Match in this one. In fact, no-one has completed more passes than Rodri, with our No.16 finding a team-mate 526 times. The closest challenger to that title is Spain defender Pau Cubarsi, who is on 441 passes. And while Rodri is pleased to see off Spain’s Iberian rivals, he’s looking for more from his team when they face Belgium in Los Angeles on Friday. “There’s a lot of room for improvement,” he began. “Now we have to rest and keep improving. We’re very similar teams, we want the ball, and we want to have personality. “It’s about small details, I’ve said it before. The first chance we had in the whole game [for Merino’s goal] came when we got inside...it’s something to learn from for the next games, we have to be sharper, because we can do a lot of damage. “In football nowadays, in the last third you have to speed things up. “When we broke their lines, we were maybe taking an extra touch. You have to turn quickly and find the players in attack. That’s how the goal came.” However, Rodri was well aware that Spain’s outstanding defensive record is due to the control and work ethic of the whole team, in and out of possession. “We were able to read the flow of the game at all times,” he said. “We wore them down, and we found our opening. “I think they played a very complete game in midfield, and I think we

input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

input.evidence_text: Дронотур Бумбокса Європою. Листопад-грудень 2026 року. Презентація максі-синглу "Живий" Кожного разу, коли перед менеджментом гурту Бумбокс стоїть завдання написати пресреліз, ми починаємо згадувати цікаві факти. Скільки концертів зіграли, скільки грошей зібрали, які пісні видали. Це база. Непогано ще пам’ятати, чи був гурт у країні, до якої ми збираємося, чи це пер О, як би хотілося написати: "Концерт гурту Бумбокс" — і як у Львові за добу продано 8000 квитків. Так-так, ми не про Львів розповідаємо, хоча теж про Європу Наші віддані слухачі, якщо ви перебуваєте за кордоном, сподіваємось, вам буде цікаво дізнатися, що після традиційної перерви на декілька місяців, Бумбокс поїде в турне від Ізраїлю до Німеччини, від Англії до Польщі, від Ірландії до Словаччини та Чехії І повеземо ми в це турне свіженький, як херсонський кавун, максі-сингл “Живий”, підсилений піснями з різних альбомів, під частину яких виросли ви, а під частину зростають ваші діти Тож ознайомтеся, будь ласка, уважно з розкладом туру, купуйте квитки заздалегідь, готуйте гроші на донати та мерч — зустрінемося в листопаді

Этот концерт — больше, чем просто выступление: это пространство, где публика сливается в едином ритме, проживая что-то настоящ MONATIK отправляется в международный концертный тур, в рамках которого посетит Великобританию! Артист влюбил миллионы слушателей в свою музыку благодаря песням “Кожний раз”, “А що?”, “Сильно”, “Кружить” и многим другим, в каждой из которых ритм занимает центральное место. input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester's economic growth has not boosted outer borough incomes, report finds Greater Manchester's economic boom has not increased household incomes across outer boroughs, with earnings remaining stagnant despite regional growth, accordi Greater Manchester's relative economic success has failed to translate into improved wages or earnings for residents in its outer boroughs, according to a new report Research by Oxford Economics has indicated that "exceptional economic growth" has not resulted in higher earnings or income growth across the wider region Economists noted that elevated levels of economic inactivity and stagnant productivity gains in areas beyond Manchester city centre have constrained household finances The report scrutinises the broader narrative unfolding ahead of Andy Burnham's anticipated move into Number 10, with the former Greater Manchester mayor having claimed credit for the growth achievements recorded in recent years He has also leveraged strong growth figures to bolster his calls for greater devolution, while pledging "good growth in every postcode", as reported by City AM

title: Gloucestershire Airport put up for sale again as bosses refuse to reveal why it's losing millions

input.lead: Retail & Consumer Transport Gloucestershire Airport put up for sale again as bosses refuse to reveal why it's losing millions The Staverton airfield is currently a loss-making site Comments Retail & Consumer Carmelo Garcia, Local Democracy Reporter and Hannah Baker South West Bus
input.summary: Retail & Consumer Transport Gloucestershire Airport put up for sale again as bosses refuse to reveal why it's losing millions The Staverton airfield is currently a loss-making site Comments Retail & Consumer Carmelo Garcia, Local Democracy Reporter and Hannah Baker South West Bus
input.evidence_text: Retail & Consumer Transport Gloucestershire Airport put up for sale again as bosses refuse to reveal why it's losing millions The Staverton airfield is currently a loss-making site Comments Retail & Consumer Carmelo Garcia, Local Democracy Reporter and Hannah Baker South West Bus Gloucestershire Airport is going back up for sale again, its joint owners have announced. Cheltenham Borough Council and Gloucester City Council confirmed on Tuesday (June 30) the Staverton site would brought back to market, with property firm Savills appointed to lead a renewed The news comes just three months after the sale of the loss-making transport hub fell through after months of negotiations. In March, a deal to offload the airport to preferred buyer Horizon Aero Group collapsed after the authorities said they could not accept the terms of the sa On Tuesday, council chiefs said they were relaunching the sale process after receiving a "number of approaches" from interested parties Councillor Rowena Hay, leader of Cheltenham Borough Council, said: ‘’We are hopeful this renewed sale process will attract the right partner for the airport’s future, which remains our key priority. We will work with partners and stakeholders to update as the new sale process pro Councillor Jeremy Hilton, leader of Gloucester City Council, said: "Gloucestershire Airport is a vital economic and aviation asset for our county and region and we must do our best for it

final_reason: Curator drop: Не относится к Манчестер.
